data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
## Summary

Add an **ablation generator**: given a winning run, automatically emit N follow-up idea proposals, each turning off exactly one component of the winning config. Feed these into the existing idea queue.

Ported idea from the (now archived) `autoresearch-ai` CLI command `researchloop-ablate`.

## Why

Right now the loop's `generate-ideas` step produces fresh ideas from scratch. After a win, the highest-value next experiments are *structured ablations* of that win ("does it still work without component X?"), not new vibes. This turns a single win into a disciplined ablation study automatically — exactly the rigor an unattended loop needs to produce trustworthy claims.

## Proposed behavior

- New `lib/ablate.ts`: parse the winning run's config flags, emit one proposal per toggleable component (each disabling a single component, all else held fixed).
- Surface as an action on a winning leaderboard entry ("Generate ablations") and/or auto-enqueue after a confirmed win in the autorun loop.
- Each generated proposal lands in the normal `ideas/` queue (same schema as `templates/autoresearch/ideas/`) so it flows through the existing implement → run → judge path.

## Source reference

`autoresearch-ai` (archived to `my-life/archive/autoresearch-ai`): `bin/researchloop-ablate.js`, and `docs/feature-ideas.md` → "Ablation generator".

## Depends on / pairs with

Best combined with the multi-seed + significance judge work (Tier 1) so ablation deltas are evaluated with proper variance, not single-run noise.
data.title: Ablation generator: turn a winning run into N single-component-off proposals
data.summary: ## Summary

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
## Summary

Add a **pre-flight cost forecast** to the loop: before any run kicks off on the GPU box, estimate wall-clock time and `$`/API cost, surface it in the dashboard, and refuse runs that exceed a budget without explicit confirmation.

Ported idea from the (now archived) `autoresearch-ai` CLI command `researchloop-forecast`.

## Why

VoidSpark runs **unattended**. An autonomous loop with no cost gate can silently burn GPU hours or API budget on a misconfigured sweep. A forecast step makes every queued run accountable before it starts, and pairs naturally with the existing `gpu-usage`, `gpu`, and `minimax-usage` / `api-budget` panels.

## Proposed behavior

- New `lib/forecast.ts` that, given a run config (model size, tokens, batch, steps) + the target box, estimates:
  - wall-clock (from declared model FLOPs + measured box throughput, or a recent comparable run)
  - `$` cost (GPU $/hr from `remote-box.json` + API token cost for agent steps)
- Wire into the run path (`app/api/run-next` / `app/api/autorun`) as a gate:
  - show the forecast in the dashboard before launch
  - if estimate > configured budget, require `--confirm` / an explicit dashboard click before proceeding
- Add a `budget` field to the project/loop config.

## Source reference

`autoresearch-ai` (archived to `my-life/archive/autoresearch-ai`): `bin/researchloop-forecast.js`, and `docs/feature-ideas.md` → "Pre-flight cost forecast".

## Out of scope

Energy/carbon tracking, multi-cloud cost modeling. Single local box only.
data.title: Pre-flight cost forecast: estimate wall-clock/$ before a run and gate over-budget runs
data.summary: ## Summary

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
`app/api/` has 27 route handlers (health, gpu, gpu-usage, ideas, orchestrate, autorun, monitor, tmux, training-curve, leaderboard, …) with no reference doc, so contributors must read each file to learn the contract.

**Proposal**
- [ ] One `docs/api.md` table: method, path, purpose, params, response shape
- [ ] Note which routes shell out to SSH/tmux (relates to #20, #39)
data.title: Document the API routes (27 endpoints) in docs/
data.summary: `app/api/` has 27 route handlers (health, gpu, gpu-usage, ideas, orchestrate, autorun, monitor, tmux, training-curve, leaderboard, …) with no reference doc, so contributors must r…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The loop launches agents, runs, and idea refills from several triggers (autopilot, manual buttons, orchestrate gate). There's no single timeline of 'what happened and why'.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Append-only audit log of actions (timestamp, action, trigger source, idea id, result)
- [ ] Surface it in Analytics as a filterable feed

**Why:** Makes stalls and surprise GPU spend debuggable after the fact.
data.title: Action/run audit log (what was triggered, when, by which path)
data.summary: The loop launches agents, runs, and idea refills from several triggers (autopilot, manual buttons, orchestrate gate). There's no single timeline of 'what happened and why'.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
There's no way to verify the rented box (`root@IP`) is reachable and correctly configured short of starting a run and watching it fail.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Settings action that runs a quick `ssh … nvidia-smi` health probe
- [ ] Report reachable / GPU present / entrypoint found, with the actual error on failure
data.title: 'Test connection' button for the remote GPU box in Settings
data.summary: There's no way to verify the rented box (`root@IP`) is reachable and correctly configured short of starting a run and watching it fail.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Action buttons (Autoresearch toggle, orchestrate, flip, generate-ideas) can be double-clicked or fired by overlapping pollers, potentially launching duplicate workers/runs.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Disable the button while its request is in flight (UI)
- [ ] Idempotency / single-flight guard server-side (lockfile or in-memory flag) so concurrent triggers collapse to one
- [ ] Surface 'already running' instead of silently double-launching
data.title: Single-flight / debounce action endpoints (autorun, orchestrate, flip)
data.summary: Action buttons (Autoresearch toggle, orchestrate, flip, generate-ideas) can be double-clicked or fired by overlapping pollers, potentially launching duplicate workers/runs.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
`globals.css` applies a `transition` to **every** element (`* { transition-property: … }`) and defines `float`/`float-delayed`/`gradient-x` keyframe animations. None of it respects users who request reduced motion.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Wrap animations/transitions in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)`
- [ ] Or disable them under `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`
data.title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion
data.summary: `globals.css` applies a `transition` to **every** element (`* { transition-property: … }`) and defines `float`/`float-delayed`/`gradient-x` keyframe animations. None of it respect…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
When SSH to the box fails, metrics silently freeze at their last value. Add an explicit staleness signal.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Track `lastSuccessfulFetch` per polled endpoint
- [ ] Dim the chip + show 'stale Xs' once it exceeds ~2× the poll interval
- [ ] Reconnect banner when the box comes back (relates to #24, #40)
data.title: Show 'data is stale' indicator when the remote box stops responding
data.summary: When SSH to the box fails, metrics silently freeze at their last value. Add an explicit staleness signal.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The cockpit metrics (workers, queue, records, flips) are informational only. Make them actionable.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Click 'Workers' → monitor panel filtered to live workers
- [ ] Click 'GPU queue' → the queued-ideas list
- [ ] Click 'Last record' → records timeline scrolled to that record
data.title: Health bar: clickable metrics that deep-link to the relevant view
data.summary: The cockpit metrics (workers, queue, records, flips) are informational only. Make them actionable.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
A runtime error in the heavy client `page.tsx` currently has no friendly fallback.

**Proposal**
- [ ] `app/error.tsx` with a reset button
- [ ] `app/not-found.tsx` styled to match the app (instead of the default Next 404)
- [ ] React error boundary around the cockpit/monitor panels
data.title: Add a global error boundary + app/error.tsx and not-found.tsx
data.summary: A runtime error in the heavy client `page.tsx` currently has no friendly fallback.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
`monitor-panel` polls `/api/tmux`/log every 3s to show terminal output, which is laggy and chatty.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Stream tmux/run logs via Server-Sent Events or WebSocket
- [ ] Tail with backpressure; drop to polling as a fallback
- [ ] Auto-scroll with a 'pause on scroll-up' affordance
data.title: Live log streaming (SSE/WebSocket) instead of 3s tmux polling
data.summary: `monitor-panel` polls `/api/tmux`/log every 3s to show terminal output, which is laggy and chatty.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The fixed-width metric chips (`min-w-[84px]`) help, but values swap from placeholder to real numbers and the Autoresearch tooltip/badge can reflow. Audit and pin heights to avoid CLS.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Reserve final height for each metric chip and badge
- [ ] Verify no layout shift when data arrives (pairs with skeletons, #28)
data.title: Reduce layout shift in the cockpit metrics row on first load
data.summary: The fixed-width metric chips (`min-w-[84px]`) help, but values swap from placeholder to real numbers and the Autoresearch tooltip/badge can reflow. Audit and pin heights to avoid…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
With no project added, the sidebar shows a flat 'No projects' + tiny '+' hint and the main area is nearly empty. First-run users get no guidance.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Prominent empty-state card in the main area: what VoidSpark does + 'Add a repo folder' CTA + link to docs
- [ ] Tie into the existing missing-`autoresearch/` warning (#11)
data.title: Onboarding empty-state for 'No projects' in the sidebar
data.summary: With no project added, the sidebar shows a flat 'No projects' + tiny '+' hint and the main area is nearly empty. First-run users get no guidance.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
API routes that `ssh` to the rented box or call `tmux` can hang if the box is unreachable, blocking the Next request (and any polling timer waiting on it). Errors are largely swallowed.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Wrap every external command with an explicit timeout (`ssh -o ConnectTimeout`, `timeout` wrapper)
- [ ] Return a structured `{ ok:false, reason }` the UI can render (pairs with #24)
- [ ] Never let a hung box stall the event loop
data.title: Harden SSH/tmux shell-outs with timeouts and structured errors
data.summary: API routes that `ssh` to the rented box or call `tmux` can hang if the box is unreachable, blocking the Next request (and any polling timer waiting on it). Errors are largely swal…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The app reads `.env.local` (remote GPU box IP/user, model selection, etc.) but there's no checked-in template, so a fresh clone can't tell what to set.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Add `.env.example` listing every variable the app/scripts read, with comments
- [ ] Reference it from README setup
data.title: Add .env.example documenting required environment / box config
data.summary: The app reads `.env.local` (remote GPU box IP/user, model selection, etc.) but there's no checked-in template, so a fresh clone can't tell what to set.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Actions like rejecting an idea or killing a worker/pane appear to fire immediately. A misclick is unrecoverable.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Lightweight confirm (or undo toast) for reject / kill / clear actions
- [ ] Prefer an 'Undo for 5s' toast over a modal where possible
data.title: Confirmation step before destructive actions (reject idea, kill worker)
data.summary: Actions like rejecting an idea or killing a worker/pane appear to fire immediately. A misclick is unrecoverable.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Track how much GPU time (and $) each idea consumed and show cost-per-record, so the loop can be judged on ROI, not just raw losses.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Record wall-clock GPU seconds per run
- [ ] Configurable $/hr in Settings
- [ ] Show cost per idea + cumulative spend in Analytics
- [ ] 'Cost per record' headline metric
data.title: Per-idea GPU cost / time tracking and ROI
data.summary: Track how much GPU time (and $) each idea consumed and show cost-per-record, so the loop can be judged on ROI, not just raw losses.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The health bar shows instantaneous numbers (GPU util, flips/hr) but no trend, so you can't tell a momentary dip from a sustained stall.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Keep a short rolling client-side history per metric
- [ ] Render tiny inline sparklines next to GPU util and flips/hr
data.title: Add sparklines for GPU util & throughput in the health bar
data.summary: The health bar shows instantaneous numbers (GPU util, flips/hr) but no trend, so you can't tell a momentary dip from a sustained stall.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
No way to get the data out of the dashboard for a paper, a Skool post, or external analysis.

**Proposal**
- [ ] 'Export' button on the records timeline and leaderboard
- [ ] CSV + JSON
- [ ] Include idea id, status, loss, baseline delta, timestamp, GPU time
data.title: Export research records & leaderboard to CSV/JSON
data.summary: No way to get the data out of the dashboard for a paper, a Skool post, or external analysis.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The Simple/Advanced toggle, selected project, and Settings choices reset on every reload.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Persist Simple/Advanced mode
- [ ] Persist last selected project
- [ ] Persist Settings (model choice, etc.)
- [ ] Rehydrate on load
data.title: Persist UI state (Simple/Advanced, selected project, settings) to localStorage
data.summary: The Simple/Advanced toggle, selected project, and Settings choices reset on every reload.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Add a Cmd/Ctrl-K command palette to jump between views (Home/Analytics/Docs/Leaderboard), switch projects, toggle Autoresearch, and open Settings without mousing through the sidebar.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Cmd-K overlay with fuzzy search over nav + actions
- [ ] Keyboard-first, Esc to close
data.title: Command palette (Cmd-K) for navigation and actions
data.summary: Add a Cmd/Ctrl-K command palette to jump between views (Home/Analytics/Docs/Leaderboard), switch projects, toggle Autoresearch, and open Settings without mousing through the sideb…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
There's a 'Last record' metric but no celebration / signal at the moment a run actually beats the baseline.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Fire a toast (and optional desktop notification) when a new best loss lands
- [ ] Briefly highlight the record row in the records timeline

**Why:** Records are the whole point of the loop; they should be impossible to miss.
data.title: Toast + notification when a new record (best loss) is set
data.summary: There's a 'Last record' metric but no celebration / signal at the moment a run actually beats the baseline.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The single biggest waste in the loop is a **paid GPU sitting idle (<5% util) while ideas are queued** — the cockpit already surfaces GPU util and queue depth, but the operator has to be watching to notice.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Web Notifications API alert when `gpu_util < 5%` AND `queue > 0` for N seconds
- [ ] Optional sound
- [ ] Permission opt-in from Settings

**Why:** Turns the dashboard from passive to actively flagging the most expensive failure mode.
data.title: Desktop/browser notification when GPU goes idle with a non-empty queue
data.summary: The single biggest waste in the loop is a **paid GPU sitting idle (<5% util) while ideas are queued** — the cockpit already surfaces GPU util and queue depth, but the operator has…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The theme is hardcoded — `<html class="dark">` in the layout and a fixed `#1f1e1d`/`#faf9f6` palette — even though `globals.css` already defines full `:root` light-mode CSS variables that go unused.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Theme toggle in the Settings dropdown (light / dark / system)
- [ ] Persist choice; respect `prefers-color-scheme` for 'system'
- [ ] Switch the hardcoded hex backgrounds to the existing `--background`/`--foreground` tokens
data.title: Add a theme toggle (light / dark / system)
data.summary: The theme is hardcoded — `<html class="dark">` in the layout and a fixed `#1f1e1d`/`#faf9f6` palette — even though `globals.css` already defines full `:root` light-mode CSS variab…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Before data arrives, the cockpit metrics (GPU util, queue, flips/hr, last record) show literal `—` and `…`, which is indistinguishable from a real 'no data' state.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Add shimmer/skeleton placeholders while the first fetch is in flight
- [ ] Only fall back to `—` once a request has actually returned empty

Pairs with #24 (error vs loading distinction).
data.title: Replace cockpit '—' / '…' placeholders with loading skeletons
data.summary: Before data arrives, the cockpit metrics (GPU util, queue, flips/hr, last record) show literal `—` and `…`, which is indistinguishable from a real 'no data' state.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The left sidebar is `hidden … md:flex`, so on phones/narrow windows there is **no navigation at all** — Home/Analytics/Documentation and the project picker are unreachable.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Hamburger button in the top bar on `<md`
- [ ] Slide-in drawer reusing the existing sidebar content
- [ ] Trap focus + close on overlay click / Esc
data.title: Add a mobile sidebar drawer (sidebar is hidden below md)
data.summary: The left sidebar is `hidden … md:flex`, so on phones/narrow windows there is **no navigation at all** — Home/Analytics/Documentation and the project picker are unreachable.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Sidebar nav items render bare glyphs (`▣`, `📊`, `📖`) inside `aria-hidden` spans next to short text. Several components (`research-records`, `analytics-view`, `markdown-*`, `documentation-view`) have **zero** `aria-*` attributes.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Audit icon-only / glyph buttons for `aria-label`
- [ ] Replace decorative emoji with lucide icons already in the dep tree, marked `aria-hidden`
- [ ] Give each view a landmark/heading

**Why:** Screen readers currently announce emoji or nothing meaningful.
data.title: Nav buttons use emoji/symbols with no accessible label
data.summary: Sidebar nav items render bare glyphs (`▣`, `📊`, `📖`) inside `aria-hidden` spans next to short text. Several components (`research-records`, `analytics-view`, `markdown-*`, `docume…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
The cockpit tooltips use `group-hover/tip:opacity-100` — they only appear on **mouse hover**. Keyboard and screen-reader users can't reach them, and the trigger elements aren't associated via `aria-describedby`.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Show tooltips on focus as well as hover (`group-focus-within`)
- [ ] Associate trigger ↔ tooltip with `aria-describedby`
- [ ] Ensure the trigger is focusable

**Why:** Accessibility + keyboard operators.
data.title: Make tooltips keyboard- and screen-reader-accessible
data.summary: The cockpit tooltips use `group-hover/tip:opacity-100` — they only appear on **mouse hover**. Keyboard and screen-reader users can't reach them, and the trigger elements aren't as…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
`app/page.tsx` makes ~46 `fetch` calls; many `.catch(() => {})` or swallow errors, so when the box is unreachable or a route 500s the UI just shows stale `—`/`…` with no signal.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Add a small toast/inline error channel
- [ ] Show a 'last updated Xs ago / stale' badge per metric when polling fails
- [ ] Distinguish 'loading' from 'errored' in the cockpit placeholders

**Why:** Operators currently can't tell 'no data yet' from 'the pipeline is broken'.
data.title: Surface fetch failures to the user instead of failing silently
data.summary: `app/page.tsx` makes ~46 `fetch` calls; many `.catch(() => {})` or swallow errors, so when the box is unreachable or a route 500s the UI just shows stale `—`/`…` with no signal.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Mixing a production `next build` and `next dev` against the same checkout leaves a poisoned `.next`: the dev manifest references `static/css/app/layout.css` / `page.css` but `.next/static/css/app/` is empty, so **both stylesheets 404 and the whole page renders unstyled** (only leftover hashed production CSS serves). Observed and fixed live by `rm -rf .next && npm run dev`.

**Proposal (pick one or more)**
- [ ] Add a `predev`/`prebuild` note or script guidance in README troubleshooting
- [ ] Document the `rm -rf .next && npm run dev` recovery one-liner
- [ ] Consider separate output dirs / a `clean` npm script

**Why:** Silent, confusing failure — page looks broken with no console error pointing at the cause.
data.title: Guard against stale .next causing an all-CSS-404 unstyled page
data.summary: Mixing a production `next build` and `next dev` against the same checkout leaves a poisoned `.next`: the dev manifest references `static/css/app/layout.css` / `page.css` but `.nex…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
No CI runs on pull requests today.

**Proposal**
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yml` running `tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, and `next build` on push/PR
- [ ] Cache node_modules / next cache for speed

**Why:** Catches type and lint breakage before merge instead of at `next dev` time.
data.title: Add GitHub Actions CI: typecheck + lint + build on PRs
data.summary: No CI runs on pull requests today.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
There are **zero test files** in the repo across ~10,500 LOC of app/components/lib.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Add Vitest + React Testing Library
- [ ] Cover the pure logic in `lib/` first (`autorun.ts`, `autoimplement.ts`, `autopilot.ts`, `projects.ts`)
- [ ] Add one API-route smoke test (e.g. `/api/health` no-project guard)
- [ ] Wire `npm test` into a GitHub Actions workflow

**Why:** The orchestration logic has subtle states (in-flight/refilling/done) and regressions are currently caught only by eyeballing the dashboard.
data.title: No automated tests anywhere (~10.5k LOC) — add Vitest + CI
data.summary: There are **zero test files** in the repo across ~10,500 LOC of app/components/lib.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Several read-only routes (`/api/gpu-usage`, `/api/health`, `/api/tmux`, `/api/monitor`) shell out to `ssh`/`nvidia-smi`/`tmux` on **every** poll. With multiple clients + multiple timers, that's a lot of duplicate shell-outs per second.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Add a tiny in-process TTL cache (e.g. 1–2s) per route so bursts of requests share one shell-out
- [ ] Add a hard timeout on the SSH calls so a hung box can't block the route

**Why:** Cuts redundant SSH load and protects the Next server from hanging on an unreachable box.
data.title: Add server-side short-TTL cache for SSH/tmux-backed API routes
data.summary: Several read-only routes (`/api/gpu-usage`, `/api/health`, `/api/tmux`, `/api/monitor`) shell out to `ssh`/`nvidia-smi`/`tmux` on **every** poll. With multiple clients + multiple…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
`app/page.tsx` is **3,647 lines** with ~46 `fetch` calls and a dozen effects in one client component. It's hard to read, review, and test.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Extract the cockpit header / health metrics row
- [ ] Extract the idea board / in-flight list
- [ ] Extract the settings dropdown
- [ ] Move data-fetching effects into reusable hooks (`useGpuUsage`, `useIdeas`, `useAutorun`, …)

Pure refactor, no behavior change.
data.title: Split the 3,647-line app/page.tsx into feature components + hooks
data.summary: `app/page.tsx` is **3,647 lines** with ~46 `fetch` calls and a dozen effects in one client component. It's hard to read, review, and test.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
All the polling loops keep firing when the dashboard tab is backgrounded, so a hidden tab keeps hammering the APIs and the remote GPU box over SSH.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Listen to `document.visibilitychange`
- [ ] Suspend all polling intervals while `document.hidden`
- [ ] Do one immediate refresh on regaining visibility

**Why:** Stops wasted requests (and SSH calls) when nobody is looking, which is most of the time for a monitoring dashboard.
data.title: Pause polling when the tab is hidden (visibilitychange)
data.summary: All the polling loops keep firing when the dashboard tab is backgrounded, so a hidden tab keeps hammering the APIs and the remote GPU box over SSH.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
`app/page.tsx` and the components run **~11 separate `setInterval` loops** (3s, 4s, 5s, 10s, 15s, 20s, 60s) that each `fetch` independently — health-bar (5s/15s/30s), monitor-panel (3s/15s), analytics-view (10s), plus 8+ in page.tsx. They don't dedupe, share data, or back off.

**Proposal**
- [ ] Introduce a single polling hook / lightweight SWR-style cache keyed by endpoint
- [ ] Dedupe concurrent requests to the same endpoint
- [ ] Centralize intervals so the cadence is configurable in one place

**Why:** Each tick on several routes shells out over SSH to the remote GPU box; 11 uncoordinated timers multiply that load and make the UI feel laggy.
data.title: Consolidate ~11 independent polling loops into one shared poller
data.summary: `app/page.tsx` and the components run **~11 separate `setInterval` loops** (3s, 4s, 5s, 10s, 15s, 20s, 60s) that each `fetch` independently — health-bar (5s/15s/30s), monitor-pane…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
README → Roadmap (M3) calls for a short demo. Add:

- A screen-capture **GIF** of the loop running (idea → implement → queue → verdict).
- A couple of dashboard screenshots (Simple + Advanced views, the GPU card, Analytics).

This is the "watch it run in minutes" on-ramp that sells the project on the public launch.
data.title: Add a "watch it run" demo GIF + screenshots to the README
data.summary: README → Roadmap (M3) calls for a short demo. Add:

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
There are four toggles whose meanings overlap and confuse users about which one to use:

- **Autoresearch** (master — runs the whole loop)
- **Autopilot**
- **GPU drainer** (the loop that SSHes to the box and picks up queued runs)
- **Auto-implement**

The new **Simple/Advanced UI** toggle hides some of this, but the underlying model is still unclear. Consolidate into a clear hierarchy (one master + clearly-scoped sub-switches), with tooltips/labels that explain the relationship, so a newcomer knows the one switch to flip.
data.title: Simplify the four overlapping automation toggles
data.summary: There are four toggles whose meanings overlap and confuse users about which one to use:

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
## Problem

The `no-autoresearch` warning (see the bug issue) only surfaces **right after the user adds the repo**. If they dismiss it, switch projects, or reload the page, a repo that's missing `autoresearch/` again just shows a silent empty dashboard.

## Proposal

Detect a missing `autoresearch/` for the **active** project too — not only at add-time:

- On project switch / dashboard load, if the active repo has no `autoresearch/`, show a persistent (dismissible) banner on the main dashboard with the same "Set it up" scaffold affordance.
- Could reuse the existing check in `lib/projects.ts` (`hasAutoresearchDir`) via a small field on the projects/active-project API response.

## Why

Makes the empty-dashboard state self-explanatory at any time, not just in the one moment after adding — closes the "my data vanished" footgun completely.

Good first issue — contained, builds on the add-time warning already in place.
data.title: Warn on missing autoresearch/ at project switch / load, not only on add
data.summary: ## Problem

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Once the adapter API stops moving, publish the CLI + orchestrator to npm. Distribution is repo-first (clone & fork) until then — publishing earlier just ships churn. README → Roadmap.

Low priority until #1/#2 stabilize the interfaces.
data.title: Distribute the CLI/orchestrator via npm (M3+)
data.summary: Once the adapter API stops moving, publish the CLI + orchestrator to npm. Distribution is repo-first (clone & fork) until then — publishing earlier just ships churn. README → Road…

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
Today a run requires a remote GPU configured via `autoresearch/remote-box.json` + SSH. Add a `local` ComputeAdapter that runs the A/B on the host machine's own GPU, so users with a local card can run the loop without renting a box.

Part of the ComputeAdapter contract (#1). Should share the launch → poll → pull shape with the SSH backend.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
A thin CLI that scaffolds `voidspark.config.ts` in a user's repo and optionally clones the example target. No global install needed.

- `npx voidspark init` — scaffold config + adapter stubs in the current repo.
- `npx voidspark init --example llm` — clone the reference kit so a newcomer watches the full loop run end to end.

Depends on the adapter seam (#1, once filed). README → Roadmap, M2. Prove it works against a **second** target repo that isn't the example.
data.title: voidspark.config.ts + `npx voidspark init` CLI (M2)
data.summary: A thin CLI that scaffolds `voidspark.config.ts` in a user's repo and optionally clones the example target. No global install needed.

data.author: vukrosic
data.body:
VoidSpark currently assumes the `autoresearch/` convention of the reference target ([universe-lm](https://github.com/vukrosic/universe-lm)). To make the engine drive **any** repo, introduce three small adapter interfaces (README → Roadmap, M1):

- **RepoAdapter** — turn an idea into runnable code: file layout, the off-by-default flag convention, a cheap build/smoke check.
- **ComputeAdapter** — where a run happens (`local`, or `ssh` to a remote box — generalizes today's `remote-box.json`).
- **Scorer** — parse the metric from a run and return WIN / NULL / FAIL against a variance band.

The reference `autoresearch/` layout (see `docs/research-repo.md`) becomes one implementation behind these interfaces. This is the core design work that unblocks M2.
data.title: Adapter seam: RepoAdapter / ComputeAdapter / Scorer (M1)
data.summary: VoidSpark currently assumes the `autoresearch/` convention of the reference target ([universe-lm](https://github.com/vukrosic/universe-lm)). To make the engine drive **any** repo,…
